hub_subtitle: Business English para as reuniões com o time Global da Sanofi, congressos médicos e a apresentação de agosto

lesson.menu_title: What's Next? -- Next Steps and Offers in the Global Meeting
lesson.menu_desc: O fim da reunião: resumir os próximos passos que você já planejou e fazer ofertas na hora -- "I'll send you the report" -- sem travar -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 7 -- What's Next?
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Emmanuele Orrico | Lesson 7 -- What's Next?

listenings.text: Emmanuele, Marc Dubois. A quick message before our upcoming meeting on Thursday. I want to talk about the next steps for Brazil, so please come with a short agenda. Two or three points, no more. And one thing from my side. After the meeting, I'll send you the summary with the actions, and I'll confirm the dates for August. If you have a question before Thursday, just write to me and I'll get back to you the same day. One last point. In the meeting, I am going to ask you for three next steps and one deadline. Think about it. That is the outcome I want. Talk to you Thursday.

Good morning, everyone. Claire Bennett here, with a short note about the upcoming Global meeting. First, the agenda. Each country is going to present the next steps for the next quarter, and Brazil, you are first again. Please keep it simple: three actions and one clear outcome. Now, two offers from my side. If you need help with the slides, write to me and I'll prepare a template for you. And if your reimbursement data is not ready, do not worry. I'll follow up with the market access team, and I'll get back to you before Friday. One more thing. After the meeting, I am going to send the final agenda, and I'll confirm the time for each country. Thank you, everyone, and see you soon.

extra_audio.text: Thank you all for the meeting. Let me give you the next steps. First, I am going to send the agenda to Paris this week. Then I'll confirm the dates for the August visit. After that, I am going to follow up with the private hospitals. And I'll get back to you with the final outcome before Friday.

items.situation: The meeting is ending. Marc asks: "So, what are your next steps?"
tips: Let me give you the next steps. First, I am going to send the agenda this week.
My next steps are simple: I am going to follow up with the hospitals, and I'll confirm the dates.

items.situation: Marc says: "I need the Brazil report today." Offer to do it now.
tips: No problem. I'll send it to you right now.
Sure. I'll send you the report after this call.

items.situation: "Can you confirm the dates for the August visit?" You are not sure yet. Make an offer.
tips: I'll check and get back to you today.
Let me confirm with my team, and I'll get back to you before Friday.

items.situation: "What is the outcome you want from the upcoming meeting?"
tips: The outcome is a clear plan for the next quarter.
I want one clear outcome: the next steps for the private market.

items.situation: Claire offers to prepare a slide template for you. Thank her and accept.
tips: Thank you for the offer. That is very kind.
Thank you, Claire. I'll use your template for the meeting.

items.situation: The reimbursement data is late. Tell Marc what you are going to do about it.
tips: I am going to follow up with the market access team.
I'll follow up with the team, and I'll commit to send it before Friday.
